→ 145	
→ 146	## Why this fork exists
→ 147	
→ 148	The upstream daemon focused on **stability** — semaphore-coordinated reads/writes, mine isolation, MCP-safe API key auth. JP's fork extended that into **production deployment patterns**:
→ 149	
→ 150	1. **Single-source-of-truth daemon for distributed Claude Code sessions.** Multiple Claude Code instances (different projects, different terminals, different machines) all routing through one daemon prevents the kind of concurrent-writer SQLite corruption that took down the canonical palace on 2026-04-24. The fork's daemon-strict mode (in [jphein/mempalace](https://github.com/jphein/mempalace)) plus this daemon's queue-and-drain plus `mempal-fast.py`'s no-import path together make that single-writer guarantee enforceable.
→ 151	
→ 152	2. **Structural snapshots for evaluation frameworks.** When SME ([multipass-structural-memory-eval](https://github.com/M0nkeyFl0wer/multipass-structural-memory-eval)) needed a structural view of the palace for diagnostics, composing it serially over MCP timed out at 60-120s. The fork added `GET /graph` so an evaluator can pull wings, rooms, tunnels, KG entities, and KG triples in one HTTP roundtrip — sub-second on a 151K-drawer palace.
→ 153	
→ 154	3. **Operational ergonomics.** `palace-mode` for switching local/remote, `deploy.sh` for the one-command release, `verify-routes.sh` for post-restart smoke testing — these are quality-of-life pieces for a daemon that's actually used day-to-day rather than just installed.
→ 155	
→ 156	The architectural argument for why those pieces survive backend swaps (chroma → pgvector, etc.) is in [`docs/event-log-frame.md`](docs/event-log-frame.md).
→ 157	
→ 158	## Architectural principles
→ 159	
→ 160	1. **Single-writer enforced by design.** SQLite + Syncthing replication + multiple writers = corruption. The daemon is the only process that writes to the palace; clients route through it via HTTP/MCP. The fork's `mempal-fast.py` and `palace-mcp-dispatch.sh` make that property hold even for hooks and MCP servers.
→ 161	
→ 162	2. **Direct sqlite reads for structural data.** `embedding_metadata` and `knowledge_graph.sqlite3` are read-only via `?mode=ro` URI for `/graph`. Bypasses the MCP read semaphore entirely, ~200× faster than the equivalent fan-out under load. Same pattern, different table, for the KG.
→ 163	
→ 164	3. **Themed messages for save/repair lifecycle.** `messages.py` returns user-facing strings in `systemMessage` so a Claude Code Stop hook surfaces `✦ N memories woven into the palace` without the client knowing the internal save/queue state.
→ 165	
→ 166	4. **Coordinated rebuild with queue-and-drain.** `/repair mode=rebuild` holds every read/write/mine semaphore slot during the destructive collection swap; `/silent-save` queues to `<palace>/palace-daemon-pending.jsonl` and replays automatically post-rebuild. No saves lost during a rebuild window.
→ 167	
→ 168	5. **Deploy and verify are the same command.** `deploy.sh` exits non-zero on sync lag, restart failure, or any verify-routes regression. The default cadence for shipping a daemon change is push + restart + verify; if any step fails the deploy aborts, leaving the previous version running.
→ 169	
→ 170	## Setup
→ 171	
→ 172	### Requirements
→ 173	- Python 3.12+
→ 174	- mempalace ≥ 3.3.2 — the [fork](https://github.com/jphein/mempalace) is recommended if you want daemon-strict hook mode (single-writer enforcement) and the warnings/sqlite-fallback search path that aren't yet on `MemPalace/mempalace develop`. Stock mempalace works for everything else; the fork-only `migrate_checkpoints_to_recovery` lifespan call is `ImportError`-gated and degrades cleanly.
→ 175	- For the local mempalace patch (`patches/mcp_server_get_collection.patch` — log + retry on `_get_collection` failure, in flight upstream as [#1286](https://github.com/MemPalace/mempalace/pull/1286)): re-apply with `scripts/apply_patches.sh` after each `pipx upgrade mempalace` until #1286 merges.
→ 176	
→ 177	### Install
→ 178	
→ 179	```bash
→ 180	git clone https://github.com/jphein/palace-daemon.git
→ 181	cd palace-daemon
→ 182	python3 -m venv venv
→ 183	source venv/bin/activate
→ 184	pip install -r requirements.txt
→ 185	```
→ 186	
→ 187	### Run (manual)
→ 188	
→ 189	```bash
→ 190	# Default: port 8085, palace at $PALACE_PATH or ~/.mempalace/palace
→ 191	python main.py
→ 192	
→ 193	# Custom palace path + auth
→ 194	PALACE_API_KEY=$(openssl rand -hex 32) python main.py --palace /mnt/raid/projects/mempalace-data/palace
→ 195	```
→ 196	
→ 197	### Run (systemd user service)
→ 198	
→ 199	```bash
→ 200	mkdir -p ~/.config/systemd/user/
→ 201	cp palace-daemon.service ~/.config/systemd/user/
→ 202	systemctl --user daemon-reload
→ 203	systemctl --user enable --now palace-daemon
→ 204	```
→ 205	
→ 206	Edit the service file to set `PALACE_API_KEY`, `MEMPALACE_PALACE`, and any custom args before installing.
→ 207	
→ 208	> [!WARNING]
→ 209	> **Never install both system AND user services.** They'll fight for port 8085 and the second instance will crash-loop. Pick one.
→ 210	
→ 211	> [!CAUTION]
→ 212	> **Don't expose port 8085 without setting `PALACE_API_KEY`.** The `/mine` endpoint accepts arbitrary filesystem paths.
→ 213	
→ 214	### Plugin client setup
→ 215	
→ 216	Use `palace-mode install` to wire the [mempalace plugin](https://github.com/MemPalace/mempalace) cache to talk to this daemon (after pointing `PALACE_DAEMON_URL` at it):
→ 217	
→ 218	```bash
→ 219	export PALACE_DAEMON_URL=http://your-host:8085
→ 220	export PALACE_API_KEY=...
→ 221	~/Projects/palace-daemon/clients/palace-mode install
→ 222	~/Projects/palace-daemon/clients/palace-mode verify
→ 223	```
→ 224	
→ 225	This installs `mempal-fast.py` as the Stop/PreCompact hook handler and `palace-mcp-dispatch.sh` as the MCP server command in the plugin cache. Idempotent — safe to re-run after plugin updates.
→ 226	
→ 227	## API
→ 228	
→ 229	| Route | Method | Purpose |
→ 230	|---|---|---|
→ 231	| `/health` | GET | Liveness + version |
→ 232	| `/search` | GET | Semantic search over `mempalace_drawers`; `limit=N`. (Stop-hook checkpoints live in `mempalace_session_recovery` — read via the `mempalace_session_recovery_read` MCP tool.) |
→ 233	| `/context` | GET | Same as `/search`, formatted for LLM prompts |
→ 234	| `/list` | GET | Query-free metadata browse — wraps `mempalace_list_drawers`. `wing=…&room=…&limit=N&offset=N`, all optional |
→ 235	| `/stats` | GET | Aggregate KG + graph + status counts |
→ 236	| `/graph` | GET | Single-shot structural snapshot (wings, rooms, tunnels, KG) — see [`docs/graph-endpoint.md`](docs/graph-endpoint.md) |
→ 237	| `/viz` | GET | Self-contained HTML status dashboard (D3 + Mermaid). Optional `?refresh=N`, `?key=…` |
→ 238	| `/repair` | POST | Coordinate repair (`mode=light\|scan\|prune\|rebuild`) |
→ 239	| `/repair/status` | GET | Current repair state + pending-writes queue depth |
→ 240	| `/silent-save` | POST | Stop-hook save path with queue-and-drain during rebuild |
→ 241	| `/memory/{id}` | DELETE | Drop a drawer — wraps `mempalace_delete_drawer` |
→ 242	| `/memory/{id}` | PATCH | Update drawer `content` / `wing` / `room` (all optional in body) — wraps `mempalace_update_drawer` |
→ 243	| `/mine` | POST | Bulk import a directory (validated absolute path only) |
→ 244	| `/flush` | POST | Force checkpoint of pending writes |
→ 245	| `/reload` | POST | Invalidate cached client + collection |
→ 246	| `/backup` | POST | SQLite snapshot to a sibling file |
→ 247	| `/mcp` | POST | MCP-protocol passthrough |
→ 248	
→ 249	All endpoints honor `X-Api-Key` when `PALACE_API_KEY` is set.
→ 250	
→ 251	## Development
→ 252	
→ 253	```bash
→ 254	# Smoke-test the running daemon
→ 255	PALACE_DAEMON_URL=http://localhost:8085 PALACE_API_KEY=... scripts/verify-routes.sh
→ 256	
→ 257	# One-command deploy (push + sync-wait + restart + verify)
→ 258	scripts/deploy.sh
→ 259	
→ 260	# Switch local Claude Code sessions between modes
→ 261	palace-mode {status,local,remote [URL],install,verify}
→ 262	```

→ 2	palace-daemon — HTTP/MCP gateway for MemPalace with concurrent access control
→ 3	
→ 4	Three semaphores govern concurrency (all tunable via PALACE_MAX_CONCURRENCY):
→ 5	  _read_sem  — up to N concurrent read-only ops (search, query, stats, …)
→ 6	  _write_sem — up to N//2 concurrent write ops (add, update, kg mutations, …)
→ 7	  _mine_sem  — one mine job at a time, independent of reads/writes
→ 8	
→ 9	Roadmap:
→ 10	  [HIGH] Verified Backups: /backup endpoint with integrity_check + smoke test retrieval.
→ 11	  [DONE] Stability: Auto-detect "Internal Error" during search and trigger index recovery.
→ 12	  [DONE] Flush: Ensure memories are checkpointed on shutdown and via /flush.
→ 13	  [HIGH] Unified Routing: Ensure all clients (including miners/compactors) use the Daemon API.
→ 14	  [MED]  Maintenance: Automate _READ_TOOLS sync with upstream mempalace.

→ 81	# ── Systemd watchdog / sd_notify ─────────────────────────────────────────────
→ 82	
→ 83	def _sd_notify(msg: str) -> None:
→ 84	    """Send a message to systemd notify socket without external dependencies."""
→ 85	    sock_path = os.environ.get("NOTIFY_SOCKET", "")
→ 86	    if not sock_path:
→ 87	        return
→ 88	    try:
→ 89	        import socket as _sock
→ 90	        with _sock.socket(_sock.AF_UNIX, _sock.SOCK_DGRAM) as s:
→ 91	            # Abstract namespace sockets use NUL prefix; systemd uses @ prefix.
→ 92	            addr = chr(0) + sock_path[1:] if sock_path.startswith("@") else sock_path
→ 93	            s.sendto(msg.encode(), addr)
→ 94	    except Exception:
→ 95	        pass
→ 96	
→ 97	
→ 98	def _watchdog_interval() -> int:
→ 99	    """Return WatchdogSec in seconds from WATCHDOG_USEC (set by systemd), or 0."""
→ 100	    try:
→ 101	        return int(os.environ.get("WATCHDOG_USEC", "0")) // 1_000_000
→ 102	    except ValueError:
→ 103	        return 0
→ 104	
→ 105	
→ 106	async def _watchdog_loop(interval_secs: int) -> None:
→ 107	    """Ping systemd watchdog at half the watchdog interval, only when palace is healthy."""
→ 108	    tick = max(10, interval_secs // 2)
→ 109	    while True:
→ 110	        await asyncio.sleep(tick)
→ 111	        try:
→ 112	            loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
→ 113	            col = await loop.run_in_executor(None, _mp._get_collection)
→ 114	            if col is not None:
→ 115	                _sd_notify("WATCHDOG=1\n")
→ 116	            else:
→ 117	                _log.warning("Watchdog: palace collection unavailable — skipping WATCHDOG=1")
→ 118	        except Exception as e:
→ 119	            _log.warning("Watchdog check failed: %s", e)

→ 122	async def _warn_if_hnsw_threads_unset() -> None:
→ 123	    """Warn if hnsw:num_threads != 1 after a collection reopen.
→ 124	
→ 125	    ChromaDB 1.5.x does not persist HNSW metadata across reopens (MemPalace
→ 126	    issue #1161). After any cache clear the collection silently reverts to
→ 127	    parallel inserts, risking SIGSEGV under concurrent writes.
→ 128	    """
→ 129	    try:
→ 130	        loop = asyncio.get_running_loop()
→ 131	        await loop.run_in_executor(None, _mp.handle_request, {
→ 132	            "jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": "hnsw-check", "method": "ping", "params": {}
→ 133	        })
→ 134	        col = _mp._collection_cache
→ 135	        meta = (col and getattr(col, "_collection", None) and
→ 136	                getattr(col._collection, "metadata", None)) or {}
→ 137	        threads = meta.get("hnsw:num_threads")
→ 138	        if threads != 1:
→ 139	            _log.warning(
→ 140	                "HNSW num_threads=%s after collection reopen — parallel inserts active. "
→ 141	                "Concurrent writes risk SIGSEGV. See MemPalace issue #1161. "
→ 142	                "Upgrade to mempalace >=3.3.4 when available.",
→ 143	                threads,
→ 144	            )
→ 145	    except Exception:
→ 146	        pass

[Read /tmp/palace-daemon-research/docs/graph-endpoint.md]
→ 1	# `GET /graph` endpoint + `list_tunnels` workaround
→ 2	
→ 3	> **Status:** SHIPPED in palace-daemon **1.6.0** (commits `2003e80`,
→ 4	> `127bf68`, `7ee7d0c`). This doc captured the original plan; the
→ 5	> sections below are kept as historical context and as the canonical
→ 6	> reference for the `list_tunnels` bug write-up that `main.py:612`
→ 7	> cites.
→ 8	>
→ 9	> Live verification 2026-04-25: `/graph` returns 200 in ~34s on the
→ 10	> first hit (cold), <1s warm, full payload (36 wings, 68 rooms,
→ 11	> 9 tunnels, 6 KG entities, 3 KG triples) on the 151K-drawer palace.
→ 12	> SME's `MemPalaceDaemonAdapter.get_graph_snapshot()` lands in 0.7s
→ 13	> against this — a 430× speedup over the MCP fallback.
→ 14	
→ 15	## Context
→ 16	
→ 17	`multipass-structural-memory-eval` (SME, JP's fork) is shipping a
→ 18	`MemPalaceDaemonAdapter` that talks to palace-daemon over HTTP for
→ 19	SME's structural diagnostics (Cat 4 / 5 / 8 / 9). The adapter ships
→ 20	working today against daemon 1.5.1 by walking four MCP tools
→ 21	(`mempalace_list_wings`, `mempalace_list_rooms` per wing,
→ 22	`mempalace_list_tunnels`, `mempalace_kg_query`), but that path is slow
→ 23	over HTTP — `list_wings` takes ~30s on the 151K-drawer palace, and
→ 24	`list_rooms` × N wings serialised over HTTP is painful.
→ 25	
→ 26	Adding a single `GET /graph` endpoint makes structural snapshots fast
→ 27	(parallel-gather server-side) and removes the only reason SME has to
→ 28	walk MCP for a structural read. The shape mirrors `/stats` exactly
→ 29	— a thin asyncio.gather over a few MCP tools, plus a direct sqlite
→ 30	read of the KG (the daemon already owns the file, so a parallel KG
→ 31	read does not violate the single-writer invariant).
→ 32	
→ 33	This was extracted from the SME-side spec at:
→ 34	`multipass-structural-memory-eval/docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-25-mempalace-daemon-adapter-design.md`
→ 35	
→ 36	The SME-side adapter does NOT block on this — it falls back to MCP
→ 37	when `/graph` 404s. This work is purely a performance + correctness
→ 38	upgrade for the daemon path.
→ 39	
→ 40	**Coordination note:** SME committed a coordination note that this
→ 41	endpoint is JP-driven, separate session/PR. Land at your own cadence;
→ 42	SME will start preferring `/graph` after a daemon version bump (1.6.0).
→ 43

→ 45	
→ 46	## Part 1 — `GET /graph` endpoint
→ 47	
→ 48	Add to `palace-daemon/main.py`, mirroring the `/stats` pattern at
→ 49	`main.py:452-461`. Bumps daemon version 1.5.1 → 1.6.0 (minor, additive).
→ 50	
→ 51	### Response shape
→ 52	
→ 53	```json
→ 54	{
→ 55	  "wings": {"<wing_name>": <drawer_count>, ...},
→ 56	  "rooms": [
→ 57	    {"wing": "<wing_name>", "rooms": {"<room_name>": <drawer_count>, ...}},
→ 58	    ...
→ 59	  ],
→ 60	  "tunnels": [
→ 61	    {"room": "<room_name>", "wings": ["<wing_a>", "<wing_b>", ...]},
→ 62	    ...
→ 63	  ],
→ 64	  "kg_entities": [
→ 65	    {"id": "<id>", "name": "<name>", "type": "<type>", "properties": {...}},
→ 66	    ...
→ 67	  ],
→ 68	  "kg_triples": [
→ 69	    {
→ 70	      "subject": "<id>",
→ 71	      "predicate": "<predicate>",
→ 72	      "object": "<id>",
→ 73	      "valid_from": "<iso8601>",
→ 74	      "valid_to": "<iso8601 or null>",
→ 75	      "confidence": <float>,
→ 76	      "source_file": "<path>"
→ 77	    },
→ 78	    ...
→ 79	  ],
→ 80	  "kg_stats": {"entities": <int>, "triples": <int>}
→ 81	}
→ 82	```
→ 83	
→ 84	### Implementation sketch
→ 85	
→ 86	```python
→ 87	@app.get("/graph")
→ 88	async def graph(x_api_key: str | None = Header(default=None)):
→ 89	    _check_auth(x_api_key)
→ 90	
→ 91	    def call(tool, args):
→ 92	        return _call({
→ 93	            "jsonrpc": "2.0", "id": 1,
→ 94	            "method": "tools/call",
→ 95	            "params": {"name": tool, "arguments": args},
→ 96	        })
→ 97	
→ 98	    # Phase 1: parallel-gather the once-per-palace tools
→ 99	    wings_resp, tunnels_resp, kg_stats_resp = await asyncio.gather(
→ 100	        call("mempalace_list_wings", {}),
→ 101	        call("mempalace_list_tunnels", {}),
→ 102	        call("mempalace_kg_stats", {}),
→ 103	    )
→ 104	    wings_payload = _unwrap(wings_resp) or {}
→ 105	    wings = wings_payload.get("wings") or {}
→ 106	
→ 107	    # Phase 2: parallel list_rooms per wing
→ 108	    room_responses = await asyncio.gather(*[
→ 109	        call("mempalace_list_rooms", {"wing": w}) for w in wings
→ 110	    ])
→ 111	    rooms = [
→ 112	        {"wing": w, "rooms": (_unwrap(r) or {}).get("rooms", {})}
→ 113	        for w, r in zip(wings, room_responses)
→ 114	    ]
→ 115	
→ 116	    # Phase 3: KG entities + triples via direct sqlite read
→ 117	    kg_entities, kg_triples = _read_kg_direct()
→ 118	
→ 119	    return {
→ 120	        "wings": wings,
→ 121	        "rooms": rooms,
→ 122	        "tunnels": _unwrap(tunnels_resp) or [],
→ 123	        "kg_entities": kg_entities,
→ 124	        "kg_triples": kg_triples,
→ 125	        "kg_stats": _unwrap(kg_stats_resp) or {},
→ 126	    }
→ 127	```
→ 128	
→ 129	### `_read_kg_direct()` helper
→ 130	
→ 131	Read-only SQLite read of `~/.mempalace/knowledge_graph.sqlite3` from
→ 132	inside the daemon process. The daemon already owns the palace file
→ 133	group, so this does not introduce a new writer — the KG is a separate
→ 134	DB file from the ChromaDB persistent client.
→ 135	
→ 136	```python
→ 137	import sqlite3
→ 138	from pathlib import Path
→ 139	
→ 140	KG_PATH = Path("~/.mempalace/knowledge_graph.sqlite3").expanduser()
→ 141	
→ 142	
→ 143	def _read_kg_direct() -> tuple[list[dict], list[dict]]:
→ 144	    if not KG_PATH.exists():
→ 145	        return [], []
→ 146	    try:
→ 147	        conn = sqlite3.connect(
→ 148	            f"file:{KG_PATH}?mode=ro", uri=True, timeout=5
→ 149	        )
→ 150	        conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
→ 151	    except sqlite3.OperationalError:
→ 152	        return [], []
→ 153	
→ 154	    entities: list[dict] = []
→ 155	    triples: list[dict] = []
→ 156	    try:
→ 157	        try:
→ 158	            for r in conn.execute(
→ 159	                "SELECT id, name, type, properties FROM entities"
→ 160	            ):
→ 161	                import json as _json
→ 162	                try:
→ 163	                    props = _json.loads(r["properties"] or "{}")
→ 164	                except Exception:
→ 165	                    props = {}
→ 166	                entities.append({
→ 167	                    "id": r["id"],
→ 168	                    "name": r["name"],
→ 169	                    "type": r["type"] or "unknown",
→ 170	                    "properties": props,
→ 171	                })
→ 172	        except sqlite3.OperationalError:
→ 173	            pass
→ 174	        try:
→ 175	            for r in conn.execute(
→ 176	                "SELECT subject, predicate, object, valid_from, valid_to, "
→ 177	                "confidence, source_file FROM triples"
→ 178	            ):
→ 179	                triples.append({
→ 180	                    "subject": r["subject"],
→ 181	                    "predicate": r["predicate"],
→ 182	                    "object": r["object"],
→ 183	                    "valid_from": r["valid_from"],
→ 184	                    "valid_to": r["valid_to"],
→ 185	                    "confidence": r["confidence"],
→ 186	                    "source_file": r["source_file"],
→ 187	                })
→ 188	        except sqlite3.OperationalError:
→ 189	            pass
→ 190	    finally:
→ 191	        conn.close()
→ 192	    return entities, triples
→ 193	```
→ 194	
→ 195	### Auth
→ 196	
→ 197	Same `_check_auth(x_api_key)` pattern as every other endpoint. No
→ 198	special permissions — `/graph` is read-only.

→ 202	## Part 2 — Fix `mempalace_list_tunnels` inconsistency
→ 203	
→ 204	Live observation 2026-04-25 against the 151K-drawer palace at
→ 205	`disks.jphe.in:8085`:
→ 206	
→ 207	- `GET /stats` → `"graph": {"tunnel_rooms": 9, ...}`
→ 208	- `POST /mcp { "name": "mempalace_list_tunnels" }` → `[]`
→ 209	
→ 210	The two should agree on what "a tunnel" is. Most likely cause: the
→ 211	list_tunnels tool's implementation has drifted from the
→ 212	`mempalace_graph_stats` tool that `/stats` consumes (different schema
→ 213	queries, stale cache, or a guard-condition that filtered to zero in
→ 214	the new MCP path). Investigation steps:
→ 215	
→ 216	1. Identify the file/function backing `mempalace_list_tunnels` in the
→ 217	   `mempalace` package (likely under `mempalace/mcp_tools/` or
→ 218	   `mempalace/palace_graph.py` — recent inspection is needed because
→ 219	   mempalace is a third-party install in pipx venv).
→ 220	2. Compare the predicate it uses to detect tunnels against the
→ 221	   predicate `mempalace_graph_stats` uses for `tunnel_rooms`.
→ 222	3. Reconcile so both produce the same set. The "tunnel = room shared
→ 223	   by ≥2 wings" definition is the historical one — anything else is
→ 224	   a regression.
→ 225	4. Add a regression test that asserts:
→ 226	   `len(list_tunnels()) == graph_stats()["tunnel_rooms"]`.
→ 227	
→ 228	If the bug is in `mempalace` itself (third-party), file an issue
→ 229	upstream OR shadow the implementation inside palace-daemon's
→ 230	`/graph` endpoint. The `/graph` response should always agree with
→ 231	`/stats.tunnel_rooms`.

→ 13	/**

→ 15	 * [SessionState.Expired], the app module is responsible for posting a
→ 16	 * notification deep-linking the user back into the WebView re-auth flow.
→ 17	 */
→ 18	@HiltWorker
→ 19	class SessionRefreshWorker @AssistedInject constructor(
→ 20	    @Assisted appContext: Context,
→ 21	    @Assisted params: WorkerParameters,
→ 22	    private val auth: AuthRepository,
→ 23	) : CoroutineWorker(appContext, params) {
→ 24	
→ 25	    override suspend fun doWork(): Result {
→ 26	        val state = auth.verifyOrExpire()
→ 27	        val tag = when (state) {
→ 28	            is SessionState.Authenticated -> "authenticated"
→ 29	            SessionState.Expired -> "expired"
→ 30	            SessionState.Anonymous -> "anonymous"
→ 31	        }
→ 32	        return Result.success(Data.Builder().putString(KEY_STATE, tag).build())
→ 33	    }
→ 34	
→ 35	    companion object {
→ 36	        const val TAG = "auth:session-refresh"
→ 37	        const val UNIQUE_NAME = "auth:session-refresh"
→ 38	        const val KEY_STATE = "state"
→ 39	    }
→ 40	}

→ 5	/**
→ 6	 * Sealed return type for every [`in`.jphe.storyvox.data.source.FictionSource] call.
→ 7	 *
→ 8	 * Forces callers to handle the failure axes that actually shape UX: not-found,
→ 9	 * rate-limited, network, auth-required, and Cloudflare challenges. Anything truly
→ 10	 * unknown should be wrapped as [NetworkError] with a `cause`.
→ 11	 */
→ 12	sealed class FictionResult<out T> {
→ 13	
→ 14	    data class Success<T>(val value: T) : FictionResult<T>()
→ 15	
→ 16	    sealed class Failure : FictionResult<Nothing>() {
→ 17	        abstract val cause: Throwable?
→ 18	        abstract val message: String
→ 19	    }
→ 20	
→ 21	    /** Resource does not exist on the source. */
→ 22	    data class NotFound(
→ 23	        override val message: String = "Not found",
→ 24	        override val cause: Throwable? = null,
→ 25	    ) : Failure()
→ 26	
→ 27	    /** HTTP 429 or equivalent. `retryAfter` may be null when the source didn't tell us. */
→ 28	    data class RateLimited(
→ 29	        val retryAfter: Duration?,
→ 30	        override val message: String = "Rate limited",
→ 31	        override val cause: Throwable? = null,
→ 32	    ) : Failure()
→ 33	
→ 34	    /** Connectivity / IO / HTTP 5xx. */
→ 35	    data class NetworkError(
→ 36	        override val message: String,
→ 37	        override val cause: Throwable? = null,
→ 38	    ) : Failure()
→ 39	
→ 40	    /** The endpoint required an authenticated session and we didn't have one. */
→ 41	    data class AuthRequired(
→ 42	        override val message: String = "Sign-in required",
→ 43	        override val cause: Throwable? = null,
→ 44	    ) : Failure()
→ 45	
→ 46	    /**
→ 47	     * The request was intercepted by Cloudflare (or similar) and needs to be
→ 48	     * resolved by a WebView. The caller is expected to escalate to a
→ 49	     * `WebViewFetcher` and retry.
→ 50	     */
→ 51	    data class Cloudflare(
→ 52	        val challengeUrl: String,
→ 53	        override val message: String = "Cloudflare challenge",
→ 54	        override val cause: Throwable? = null,
→ 55	    ) : Failure()
→ 56	}

→ 58	/** Map success while passing failures through unchanged. */
→ 59	inline fun <T, R> FictionResult<T>.map(transform: (T) -> R): FictionResult<R> =
→ 60	    when (this) {
→ 61	        is FictionResult.Success -> FictionResult.Success(transform(value))
→ 62	        is FictionResult.Failure -> this
→ 63	    }
→ 64	
→ 65	/** Run a side effect on success; return the original result. */
→ 66	inline fun <T> FictionResult<T>.onSuccess(block: (T) -> Unit): FictionResult<T> {
→ 67	    if (this is FictionResult.Success) block(value)
→ 68	    return this
→ 69	}
→ 70	
→ 71	/** Run a side effect on failure; return the original result. */
→ 72	inline fun <T> FictionResult<T>.onFailure(block: (FictionResult.Failure) -> Unit): FictionResult<T> {
→ 73	    if (this is FictionResult.Failure) block(this)
→ 74	    return this
→ 75	}

→ 98	        // SearchQuery filter fields (genres, tags, statuses,

→ 100	        // are ignored.
→ 101	        //
→ 102	        // The GitHub filter sheet (step 8c) composes its own
→ 103	        // qualifier-laden query (stars:, language:, pushed:, sort:,
→ 104	        // and possibly its own topic:) and stuffs it into
→ 105	        // SearchQuery.term. Skip our default topic prefix when the

→ 107	        // double-up — the filter layer is more authoritative when
→ 108	        // it's chosen to override.
→ 109	        val term = query.term.trim()

→ 111	            if (!term.contains("topic:", ignoreCase = true)) {
→ 112	                append("(topic:fiction OR topic:fanfiction OR topic:webnovel)")
→ 113	                if (term.isNotEmpty()) append(' ')
→ 114	            }
→ 115	            if (term.isNotEmpty()) append(term)

→ 117	
→ 118	        return when (val r = api.searchRepositories(gh, page = query.page)) {
→ 119	            is GitHubApiResult.Success -> {
→ 120	                val items = r.value.items.map { it.toFictionSummary() }
→ 121	                FictionResult.Success(
→ 122	                    ListPage(
→ 123	                        items = items,

→ 125	                        // GitHub search caps at 1000 results across all
→ 126	                        // pages; signal end-of-list when items < per_page
→ 127	                        // OR we've reached the cap.
→ 128	                        hasNext = items.isNotEmpty() && items.size >= 20 && query.page < 50,
→ 129	                    ),
→ 130	                )

→ 132	            is GitHubApiResult.NotFound -> FictionResult.Success(
→ 133	                ListPage(items = emptyList(), page = query.page, hasNext = false),
→ 134	            )
→ 135	            is GitHubApiResult.RateLimited -> FictionResult.RateLimited(
→ 136	                retryAfter = r.retryAfterSeconds?.let { it.toDuration(DurationUnit.SECONDS) },
→ 137	            )
→ 138	            is GitHubApiResult.HttpError -> FictionResult.NetworkError(
→ 139	                message = "GitHub error ${r.code}: ${r.message}",
→ 140	            )
→ 141	            is GitHubApiResult.NetworkError -> FictionResult.NetworkError(
→ 142	                message = "Could not reach GitHub",
→ 143	                cause = r.cause,
→ 144	            )

→ 174	    override suspend fun fictionDetail(fictionId: String): FictionResult<FictionDetail> {
→ 175	        val coords = parseFictionId(fictionId)

→ 183	            is GitHubApiResult.Success -> r.value
→ 184	            is GitHubApiResult.NotFound -> return FictionResult.NotFound(message = r.message)
→ 185	            is GitHubApiResult.RateLimited -> return FictionResult.RateLimited(
→ 186	                retryAfter = r.retryAfterSeconds?.let { it.toDuration(DurationUnit.SECONDS) },
→ 187	            )
→ 188	            is GitHubApiResult.HttpError -> return FictionResult.NetworkError(
→ 189	                message = "GitHub error ${r.code}: ${r.message}",
→ 190	            )
→ 191	            is GitHubApiResult.NetworkError -> return FictionResult.NetworkError(
→ 192	                message = "Could not reach GitHub",
→ 193	                cause = r.cause,
→ 194	            )
→ 195	            is GitHubApiResult.ParseError -> return FictionResult.NetworkError(
→ 196	                message = "Malformed repo response",
→ 197	                cause = r.cause,
→ 198	            )
→ 199	        }
→ 200	
→ 201	        val branch = ghRepo.defaultBranch
→ 202	
→ 203	        // Manifest candidates. Each is best-effort: a 404 just means
→ 204	        // the author didn't author that file. Anything else (rate

→ 211	        storyvoxJsonOpt.failureOrNull?.let { return it }

→ 214	        summaryMdOpt.failureOrNull?.let { return it }
→ 215	
→ 216	        val bookToml = bookTomlOpt.text
→ 217	        val storyvoxJson = storyvoxJsonOpt.text
→ 218	        val summaryMd = summaryMdOpt.text
→ 219	
→ 220	        val bareRepoPaths = if (summaryMd.isNullOrBlank()) {
→ 221	            listBareRepoPaths(owner, repo, branch, srcDirGuess)
→ 222	        } else {
→ 223	            emptyList()

→ 226	        val manifest = ManifestParser.parse(
→ 227	            fictionId = fictionId,
→ 228	            bookToml = bookToml,

→ 230	            summaryMd = summaryMd,
→ 231	            bareRepoPaths = bareRepoPaths,

→ 293	    /**
→ 294	     * Cheap-poll revision token: head commit SHA on the repo's default
→ 295	     * branch. The poll worker compares against the previously-stored
→ 296	     * token and skips the heavier `fictionDetail` round-trip when they
→ 297	     * match. Step 9 in the GitHub-source spec.
→ 298	     *
→ 299	     * Two API calls per check (`getRepo` for `default_branch` then
→ 300	     * `/commits?sha={branch}&per_page=1` for the head SHA), against a
→ 301	     * full `fictionDetail` of repo + book.toml + storyvox.json +
→ 302	     * SUMMARY.md (4-5 calls + parsing). Net win even if no skip-eligible
→ 303	     * fictions exist yet, because the parsing alone is the dominant
→ 304	     * cost.
→ 305	     *
→ 306	     * Failures (network, rate-limit, 404) come back as the equivalent
→ 307	     * `FictionResult.Failure` variants; the worker treats those as
→ 308	     * "fall back to the full path" rather than aborting the whole poll.
→ 309	     */
→ 310	    override suspend fun latestRevisionToken(fictionId: String): FictionResult<String?> {
→ 311	        val coords = parseFictionId(fictionId)
→ 312	            ?: return FictionResult.NotFound(message = "Not a GitHub fiction id: $fictionId")
→ 313	        val (owner, repo) = coords
→ 314	
→ 315	        val branch = when (val r = api.getRepo(owner, repo)) {
→ 316	            is GitHubApiResult.Success -> r.value.defaultBranch
→ 317	            is GitHubApiResult.NotFound -> return FictionResult.NotFound(message = r.message)
→ 318	            is GitHubApiResult.RateLimited -> return FictionResult.RateLimited(
→ 319	                retryAfter = r.retryAfterSeconds?.let { it.toDuration(DurationUnit.SECONDS) },
→ 320	            )
→ 321	            is GitHubApiResult.NetworkError -> return FictionResult.NetworkError(
→ 322	                message = "Could not reach GitHub",
→ 323	                cause = r.cause,
→ 324	            )
→ 325	            is GitHubApiResult.HttpError -> return FictionResult.NetworkError(
→ 326	                message = "GitHub error ${r.code}: ${r.message}",
→ 327	            )
→ 328	            is GitHubApiResult.ParseError -> return FictionResult.NetworkError(
→ 329	                message = "Malformed repo response",
→ 330	                cause = r.cause,
→ 331	            )
→ 332	        }
→ 333	
→ 334	        return when (val r = api.getHeadCommit(owner, repo, branch)) {
→ 335	            is GitHubApiResult.Success -> {
→ 336	                // Empty list means the branch has no commits yet — treat
→ 337	                // as "no revision known", caller falls back to the full
→ 338	                // path. A real repo always has at least one commit.
→ 339	                FictionResult.Success(r.value.firstOrNull()?.sha)

→ 341	            is GitHubApiResult.NotFound -> FictionResult.NotFound(message = r.message)
→ 342	            is GitHubApiResult.RateLimited -> FictionResult.RateLimited(
→ 343	                retryAfter = r.retryAfterSeconds?.let { it.toDuration(DurationUnit.SECONDS) },
→ 344	            )
→ 345	            is GitHubApiResult.NetworkError -> FictionResult.NetworkError(
→ 346	                message = "Could not reach GitHub",
→ 347	                cause = r.cause,
→ 348	            )
→ 349	            is GitHubApiResult.HttpError -> FictionResult.NetworkError(
→ 350	                message = "GitHub error ${r.code}: ${r.message}",
→ 351	            )
→ 352	            is GitHubApiResult.ParseError -> FictionResult.NetworkError(
→ 353	                message = "Malformed commits response",
→ 354	                cause = r.cause,
→ 355	            )

→ 357	    }

→ 401	     * Best-effort `[book].src` extraction for the SUMMARY.md path

→ 403	     * through ManifestParser because we need it before the parser
→ 404	     * runs (we feed it the SUMMARY contents as one of its inputs).

→ 413	    /**
→ 414	     * Listing for the bare-repo fallback: try `chapters/` first, then
→ 415	     * the manifest-claimed src dir. Returns an empty list on any
→ 416	     * failure — bare-repo is itself a fallback, so a missing dir just
→ 417	     * means "nothing to fall back to."

→ 419	    private suspend fun listBareRepoPaths(
→ 420	        owner: String,
→ 421	        repo: String,
→ 422	        ref: String,
→ 423	        srcDir: String,
→ 424	    ): List<String> {
→ 425	        val candidates = listOf("chapters", srcDir).distinct()

→ 430	                if (files.isNotEmpty()) return files
→ 431	            }
→ 432	        }
→ 433	        return emptyList()
→ 434	    }

→ 479	    private fun parseStatus(raw: String?, repo: GhRepo): FictionStatus = when {
→ 480	        raw?.equals("completed", ignoreCase = true) == true -> FictionStatus.COMPLETED
→ 481	        raw?.equals("hiatus", ignoreCase = true) == true -> FictionStatus.HIATUS
→ 482	        raw?.equals("dropped", ignoreCase = true) == true -> FictionStatus.DROPPED

→ 487	    /** `github:owner/repo` → `(owner, repo)` or null. */
→ 488	    private fun parseFictionId(fictionId: String): Pair<String, String>? {
→ 489	        val stripped = fictionId.removePrefix("${SourceIds.GITHUB}:")
→ 490	        if (stripped == fictionId) return null
→ 491	        val slash = stripped.indexOf('/')
→ 492	        if (slash <= 0 || slash == stripped.length - 1) return null
→ 493	        return stripped.substring(0, slash) to stripped.substring(slash + 1)
→ 494	    }

→ 3	/**
→ 4	 * Pure-JVM URL → (sourceId, fictionId) dispatcher used by the
→ 5	 * paste-anything add-fiction flow. Lives in :core-data so source modules
→ 6	 * stay leaves of the dependency graph; each pattern is owned here, not
→ 7	 * lifted from the source modules.
→ 8	 *
→ 9	 * Returns null when no pattern matches. Recognised-but-unsupported
→ 10	 * sources (GitHub today) still return a [Match] — the repository layer
→ 11	 * decides whether to honour or reject the routing.
→ 12	 */
→ 13	object UrlRouter {
→ 14	
→ 15	    /** `https://www.royalroad.com/fiction/{id}` and `/fiction/{id}/.../chapter/...`. */
→ 16	    private val ROYALROAD_PATTERN = Regex(
→ 17	        """^https?://(?:www\.)?royalroad\.com/fiction/(\d+)(?:/.*)?$""",
→ 18	        RegexOption.IGNORE_CASE,
→ 19	    )
→ 20	
→ 21	    /** `https://github.com/{owner}/{repo}` and `/tree/{branch}` etc. */
→ 22	    private val GITHUB_HTTPS_PATTERN = Regex(
→ 23	        """^https?://github\.com/([\w.-]+)/([\w.-]+?)(?:\.git)?(?:/.*)?$""",
→ 24	        RegexOption.IGNORE_CASE,
→ 25	    )
→ 26	
→ 27	    /** Short form `github:owner/repo` or bare `owner/repo` (no scheme, no slashes elsewhere). */
→ 28	    private val GITHUB_SHORT_PATTERN = Regex(
→ 29	        """^(?:github:)?([\w.-]+)/([\w.-]+?)(?:\.git)?$""",
→ 30	    )
→ 31	
→ 32	    data class Match(val sourceId: String, val fictionId: String)
→ 33	
→ 34	    fun route(input: String): Match? {
→ 35	        val trimmed = input.trim()
→ 36	        if (trimmed.isEmpty()) return null
→ 37	
→ 38	        ROYALROAD_PATTERN.matchEntire(trimmed)?.let { m ->
→ 39	            return Match(SourceIds.ROYAL_ROAD, m.groupValues[1])
→ 40	        }
→ 41	
→ 42	        GITHUB_HTTPS_PATTERN.matchEntire(trimmed)?.let { m ->
→ 43	            return Match(SourceIds.GITHUB, "${SourceIds.GITHUB}:${m.groupValues[1].lowercase()}/${m.groupValues[2].lowercase()}")
→ 44	        }
→ 45	
→ 46	        // Reject ambiguous short form on URLs that look like a full path
→ 47	        // (anything containing `://` or more than one `/`). The short
→ 48	        // pattern only matches `owner/repo` cleanly.
→ 49	        if ("://" !in trimmed && trimmed.count { it == '/' } == 1) {
→ 50	            GITHUB_SHORT_PATTERN.matchEntire(trimmed)?.let { m ->
→ 51	                return Match(SourceIds.GITHUB, "${SourceIds.GITHUB}:${m.groupValues[1].lowercase()}/${m.groupValues[2].lowercase()}")
→ 52	            }
→ 53	        }
→ 54	
→ 55	        return null
→ 56	    }
→ 57	}

→ /**
→  * Hilt graph for `:core-data`. Note that `FictionSource` itself is NOT bound
→  * here — it lives in `:source-royalroad` (Oneiros) under its own
→  * `@InstallIn(SingletonComponent::class)` module. We just inject it into our
→  * repository impls.
→  */
→ @Module
→ @InstallIn(SingletonComponent::class)
→ object DataModule {
→ 
→     @Provides
→     @Singleton
→     fun provideDb(@ApplicationContext ctx: Context): StoryvoxDatabase =
→         Room.databaseBuilder(ctx, StoryvoxDatabase::class.java, StoryvoxDatabase.NAME)
→             .addMigrations(*ALL_MIGRATIONS)
→             .build()
→ 
→     @Provides fun fictionDao(db: StoryvoxDatabase): FictionDao = db.fictionDao()
→     @Provides fun chapterDao(db: StoryvoxDatabase): ChapterDao = db.chapterDao()
→     @Provides fun playbackDao(db: StoryvoxDatabase): PlaybackDao = db.playbackDao()
→     @Provides fun authDao(db: StoryvoxDatabase): AuthDao = db.authDao()
→ 
→     @Provides
→     @Singleton
→     fun provideEncryptedPrefs(@ApplicationContext ctx: Context): SharedPreferences {
→         val masterKey = MasterKey.Builder(ctx)
→             .setKeyScheme(MasterKey.KeyScheme.AES256_GCM)
→             .build()
→         return EncryptedSharedPreferences.create(
→             ctx,
→             "storyvox.secrets",
→             masterKey,
→             EncryptedSharedPreferences.PrefKeyEncryptionScheme.AES256_SIV,
→             EncryptedSharedPreferences.PrefValueEncryptionScheme.AES256_GCM,
→         )
→     }
→ }
→ 
→ @Module
→ @InstallIn(SingletonComponent::class)
→ abstract class RepositoryBindings {
→ 
→     @Binds @Singleton
→     abstract fun bindFictionRepository(impl: FictionRepositoryImpl): FictionRepository
→ 
→     @Binds @Singleton
→     abstract fun bindChapterRepository(impl: ChapterRepositoryImpl): ChapterRepository
→ 
→     @Binds @Singleton
→     abstract fun bindAuthRepository(impl: AuthRepositoryImpl): AuthRepository
→ 
→     @Binds @Singleton
→     abstract fun bindPlaybackPositionRepository(
→         impl: PlaybackPositionRepositoryImpl,
→     ): PlaybackPositionRepository
→ 
→     @Binds @Singleton
→     abstract fun bindLibraryRepository(impl: LibraryRepositoryImpl): LibraryRepository
→ 
→     @Binds @Singleton
→     abstract fun bindFollowsRepository(impl: FollowsRepositoryImpl): FollowsRepository
→ 
→     @Binds @Singleton
→     abstract fun bindChapterDownloadScheduler(
→         impl: WorkManagerChapterDownloadScheduler,
